title: picea engelmannii subsp. mohinorsensis
intro: Picea engelmannii subsp. mohinorsensis, also known as Engelmann spruce, is a subspecies of evergreen tree native to the high-altitude mountains of North America. The medicinal properties of Picea engelmannii subsp. mohinorsensis include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful for treating a variety of health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and promote immune function. Herbal preparations of Picea engelmannii subsp. mohinorsensis include essential oils, tinctures, and infused teas, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
